subject: Ecology
title: Earthworms: Ecosystem Engineers
description: A quick win: understand Earthworms: Ecosystem Engineers.

hook.text: Charles Darwin spent 39 years studying earthworms and called them the most important animal.

buildup.text: Worms eat dead leaves and soil, then excrete nutrient-rich castings that plants love.

discovery.text: Their tunnels aerate soil, improve drainage, and create paths for roots to follow.

twist.text: In some ecosystems, worms move more soil than any geological force—tons per hectare per year.

climax.text: But invasive earthworms in northern forests destroy leaf litter that native species depend on.

punchline.text: Worms build the soil world one tunnel at a time.

quiz.question: Why are earthworm tunnels important for soil?
options: They aerate soil and improve drainage
